commit.message:
fix: install WASM host Qt to same dir as wasm_singlethread so qmake wrapper resolves

The wasm_singlethread qmake wrapper script calls the sibling path
Qt/6.6.3/gcc_64/bin/qmake6. When gcc_64 is installed to a separate
dir (qt-host), that path doesn't exist and qmake exits 127.

Remove the dir: override on the host Qt install so both wasm_singlethread
and gcc_64 land under the same default base directory, making the
relative sibling path resolvable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix fsyncDir panic on tmpfs and replicatePut corruption race (#6)

Two fixes for failing tests:

### 1. `fsyncDir` panic on tmpfs

`fsyncDir` panics on tmpfs (i.e. `/tmp`) because Zig's
`std.posix.fsync()` maps `EINVAL` to `unreachable` rather than a
catchable error. This crashes both the unit test for `copyFileWithSync`
and the E2E replication test (process aborts before `.sum` sidecar is
copied).

- Replace `posix.fsync(dir.fd) catch {}` with `_ =
std.posix.system.fsync(dir.fd)` to use the raw syscall directly,
bypassing the `unreachable` trap

```zig
// Before: panics on EINVAL/EBADF/EROFS (unreachable, not catchable)
posix.fsync(dir.fd) catch {};

// After: raw syscall returns int, safely discarded
_ = std.posix.system.fsync(dir.fd);
```

Cross-platform: resolves to raw Linux syscall or C library `fsync()` on
macOS via `std.posix.system`.

### 2. `replicatePut` corruption race condition

When the process is killed between replication completing and log
truncation finishing, stale replication log entries get replayed on
remount. This causes `replicatePut` to copy the corrupted backing file
over the good replica, making the scrub unable to repair (it sees
"replica also corrupt"). This caused the E2E `self_healing` test to fail
intermittently.

- Add a pre-replication integrity check in `replicatePut` that computes
the BLAKE3 hash of the backing file and compares it against the stored
`.sum`. If they don't match, replication is skipped so the scrub can
repair from the still-good replica.

<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Feature: Add image resize and crop capabilities via query
parameters</issue_title>
> <issue_description>### Background
> With the migration to `libvips` (v3.0), Manael can now perform
extremely fast and memory-efficient image resizing and cropping. This is
a highly requested feature (originally discussed in 2018) that will turn
Manael into a fully-fledged image processing proxy.
> 
> To make it easy for frontend developers (e.g., for `srcset` support),
we should adopt a query-string based approach. However, allowing
arbitrary sizes poses a severe security risk (Cache Poisoning / Denial
of Wallet attacks). Therefore, we must implement strict dimension limits
and an allowed-list (whitelist) mechanism.
> 
> ### Proposal
> Allow clients to request resized images using query parameters like
`?w=1280&h=720&fit=cover`.
> To secure the endpoint, introduce configuration options (environment
variables) to enforce maximum dimensions and optionally restrict
resizing to a specific set of allowed sizes.
> 
> ### Query Parameters
> - `w` (width): Target width in pixels.
> - `h` (height): Target height in pixels.
> - `fit`: How the image should be resized to fit the target dimensions
(e.g., `cover`, `contain`, `scale-down`).
> - *Note: If only `w` or `h` is provided, the original aspect ratio
should be maintained.*
> 
> ### Security & Limits (Configuration)
> We should add the following options/environment variables to
`ProxyOptions`:
> 
> 1. **Max Dimension Limits:**
>    - `MANAEL_MAX_RESIZE_WIDTH` (e.g., `4000`)
>    - `MANAEL_MAX_RESIZE_HEIGHT` (e.g., `4000`)
> - Requests exceeding these dimensions should be rejected or clamped.
> 
> 2. **Allowed Sizes (Whitelist):**
>    - `MANAEL_ALLOWED_WIDTHS` (e.g., `320,640,1280`)
>    - `MANAEL_ALLOWED_HEIGHTS` (e.g., `240,480,720`)
>    - Accepts a comma-separated list of integers.
> - If configured, any request containing a `w` or `h` value not present
in these lists will be rejected with a `400 Bad Request` or `403
Forbidden`. If left empty, any size up to the max limits is allowed.
> 
> ### Example Request
> ```http
> GET /photo.jpeg?w=640&h=480&fit=cover HTTP/1.1
> Accept: image/avif, image/webp
> ```</issue_description>

commit.message:
feat: DOM integration layer (closes #311) (#372)

Add DOM integration infrastructure bridging the Stator JS engine with web
platform DOM APIs:

- DomObjectWrap: wraps C++ DOM nodes as JS objects with internal fields
  for storing opaque embedder pointers (up to 16 slots)
- NamedPropertyHandlerConfig: intercept named property access (getter,
  setter, query, deleter, enumerator) for DOM attributes like element.id
- IndexedPropertyHandlerConfig: intercept indexed property access
  (getter, setter, query, length) for collections like NodeList[0]
- DomWeakRef: weak reference with invoke-on-GC callback to prevent leaks
  when JS wrappers are collected
- C-ABI FFI surface (stator_dom_*) for all DOM integration types

commit.message:
Expand crypto benchmarks with comprehensive OpenSSL tests

Add symmetric ciphers (AES-CBC, AES-CTR, ChaCha20), OpenSSL speed
throughput tests (AES-GCM, ChaCha20-Poly1305), asymmetric operations
(RSA-2048/4096, ECDSA P-256, Ed25519), HMAC variants, key derivation
(PBKDF2), and separate OpenSSL vs coreutils digest comparison.

Fix duplicate AES-CBC bug (was labeled GCM but ran CBC twice).
Dynamic algorithm detection via openssl enc/dgst -list.

These benchmarks are especially sensitive to compiler flags (-march,
LTO, -O level) since OpenSSL crypto primitives rely on hardware
acceleration (AES-NI, SHA-NI) and compiler auto-vectorization.

commit.message:
dts: increase BCM4330 pwrseq delay to 500ms and cap SDIO to 25MHz

Increase post-power-on-delay-ms from 200 to 500 to give the BCM4330
more time to stabilize after power-on before the reset GPIO is released.

Add max-frequency = <25000000> to mmc1 to cap the SDIO clock at 25 MHz.
BCM4330 supports 50 MHz in theory but is unreliable at that speed on this
hardware, causing intermittent 'fatal err update clk timeout' failures
followed by 'mmc1: Failed to initialize a non-removable card' at boot.

commit.message:
Add TMF 637 Product Inventory traffic simulator

Bash script using curl to simulate various traffic patterns against
a TMF 637 Product Inventory Management API v5 (local server).

Scenarios:
- crud:    Full create/retrieve/patch/delete lifecycle
- queries: List & query patterns (filters, pagination, field selection, sorting)
- errors:  Error and edge-case traffic (4xx/5xx)
- events:  Event subscription (hub) register & unregister
- notify:  Notification listener endpoint calls (all 5 event types)
- states:  Full product state lifecycle transitions
- burst:   Concurrent parallel request bursts (10–15 simultaneous requests)

Products created during a run are tracked and cleaned up automatically.
Use --no-cleanup to keep them, --fast to skip delays between requests.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Remove host_vars and group_vars from version control

These directories contain machine-specific data (hostnames, build
profiles, USE flags) generated by the collect_use_flags role. They are
not needed for the benchmark suite and expose real machine names.

The collect_use_flags role regenerates them on demand from live hosts.
The benchmark suite uses default({}) fallbacks for these variables.

feat: Blink bindings scaffold (closes #310) (#370)

Add ObjectTemplate, TryCatch, and typed value wrapper infrastructure
for the C++ ↔ Rust FFI layer used by Chromium's Blink renderer.

Rust FFI (stator_ffi):
- StatorObjectTemplate: create/destroy/set/new_instance + internal fields
- StatorTryCatch: create/destroy/has_caught/exception/reset
- stator_isolate_has_pending_exception / clear_pending_exception

C++ wrappers (v8_compat.h):
- v8::TryCatch — exception handling across FFI boundary
- v8::ObjectTemplate — object creation templates for DOM prototypes
- v8::EscapableHandleScope — structural API compatibility
- v8::Number, v8::Integer, v8::Boolean — typed value subclasses
- v8::Object, v8::Array — V8-compatible object/array wrappers

All new types exported via cbindgen.toml and auto-generated stator.h.
